description:
Goal: publish a stable closed emission inventory from AbiReadyProgram. Signal: interpreter/JIT/AOT adapters can enumerate executable entries, callable entries, call edges, return contracts, extern marshal facts, and effects without building their own reachability or spec registry.

Change:
- Add EmissionReadyProgram with stable executable and callable-entry inventory derived from AbiReadyProgram.
- Assign any dense ordinals as emission-local ids over Compiler2 keys; never make old SpecId or SpecRegistry semantic authority.
- Keep backend-specific wrapper names, Cranelift signatures, continuation classification, and native/cont sets out of Compiler2 facts.
- Add the job/fact transition AbiReady(root) -> EmissionReady(root).

Why:
- Emission needs a deterministic inventory, but semantic identity remains Compiler2 ids and artifact keys. Dense ids are for emission bookkeeping only.

Checks:
- Quicksort inventory includes only the closed executable frontier and excludes foo/0.
- Enum.reduce inventory includes the required callable entry entries.
- Re-running unchanged input does not bump revisions for identical EmissionReadyProgram state.
- Tests assert inventory keys and opaque facts, not formatted symbol strings.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: derive EmissionReadyProgram inventory
